fields.description:
## Context

AI agents running the DSO test gate today endure significant friction from two legacy harnesses: `test-batched.sh` — a RUN-block handshake loop where the orchestrator re-invokes the same command ~13 times to complete a 337-test suite, re-printing progress on every pass — and `nohup-launch.sh` / `nohup-poll.sh` — a PID-registry harness with heartbeat-based stall detection and a process budget. Both were built as workarounds for Claude Code's ~73-second Bash-tool default ceiling and the exit-144 SIGTERM propagation bug (anthropics/claude-code#45717, currently unresolved). The Claude Code ecosystem has since converged on `Bash(run_in_background: true)` + `BashOutput(bash_id)` as the proven pattern: it decouples the background process group from Claude's main turn (so child SIGTERM does not kill the parent), returns only new output since the last drain (bounded context cost), and collapses the multi-turn handshake into one dispatch + incremental drain. Three per-language runner scripts (`bash-runner.sh`, `pytest-runner.sh`, `node-runner.sh`) under `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/` exist solely to support batched-resume; once batching goes away, so do they. Epic 87e9-0bc8 delivers the polyglot `commands.test_runner` / `commands.test_file_runner` config keys that this epic's launcher consumes. Primary beneficiaries: every DSO-consumer agent spending dozens of tool calls and thousands of tokens per commit on test-batched resume cycles.

## Success Criteria

- `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` exists and exposes three subcommands via the `.claude/scripts/dso test-run [start|status|kill]` shim. `start` reads `commands.test_runner` (defined by epic 87e9-0bc8) and launches it as a detached child, writing combined stdout+stderr to `<state_dir>/progress.log`, PID to `<state_dir>/pid`, and — on completion — exit code to `<state_dir>/exit-code` **via atomic write-then-rename** (write to `<state_dir>/.exit-code.tmp`, `mv` to `<state_dir>/exit-code`, then `touch <state_dir>/done` last). `status` prints one of `running <pid>`, `done <exit-code>`, or `idle`. `kill` runs identity-verified liveness check (see below) before terminating + clears state via SIGTERM, grace period from `commands.test_kill_grace_seconds` (default 30s to accommodate pytest teardown / coverage finalization), then SIGKILL.
- Per-session isolation: the DSO session-start hook captures the Claude Code `session_id` from hook input, **sanitizes it to `[a-f0-9-]+` (rejecting any input containing `/`, `..`, whitespace, or path-traversal sequences — fall back to a hash of the raw session_id when the input is non-conforming)**, and writes it to `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/.current-session`. The launcher reads that file to scope all state under `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/<session_id>/`. Two Claude sessions running in different worktrees (or the same worktree) can invoke `test-run start` simultaneously without any shared-state collision. **When `.current-session` is missing (CI runs, session-start hook failed to fire, upgraded plugin with stale hooks), the launcher falls back to a deterministic key derived from `$GITHUB_RUN_ID`, `$CI_JOB_ID`, or a cwd-hash in that precedence order — never silently using a shared default.**
- Same-session duplicate-launch guard with **identity-verified liveness**: when `test-run start` is invoked while `<state_dir>/pid` exists, the launcher tests both (a) `kill -0 <pid>` succeeds AND (b) the process's current cmdline matches the `<state_dir>/cmdline` snapshot written at launch time (guards against PID reuse by an unrelated process). If both match, exit non-zero with an error message whose body contains the exact override command string (`.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force`). If `kill -0` succeeds but cmdline does not match, auto-clear the state dir (stale-state recovery — PID was reused).
- Atomic duplicate-launch guard: the start subcommand acquires an `flock` on `<state_dir>/.launch-lock` before the liveness check and holds it through the pid write, preventing two rapid-fire `test-run start` invocations in the same session from both passing the check and spawning parallel orphans.
- Stale-state auto-recovery: when the launcher sees `<state_dir>` but `<state_dir>/pid` is missing, or `kill -0 <pid>` fails, or cmdline does not match the snapshot, the launcher silently clears the state dir and proceeds without requiring the override flag. No error, no agent friction, no ticket for stuck state.
- Override flag: `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force` unconditionally terminates the recorded PID via the same SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL ladder the `kill` subcommand uses, but only after confirming the cmdline snapshot matches (refuses to kill a process whose cmdline does not match — returns a clear error). Clears the state dir and starts the new run.
- Orchestrator invocation contract — documented in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md: the orchestrator invokes `test-run start` via `Bash(run_in_background: true)` to get a `bash_id`. `BashOutput(bash_id)` is used to drain progress streaming only — **it is NOT the completion signal**. Completion is determined by polling for `<state_dir>/done`; on completion the orchestrator reads `<state_dir>/exit-code` as authoritative. This contract is documented with an explicit "do not treat BashOutput return-empty as completion" note.
- Legacy harness removal: `test-batched.sh`, `nohup-launch.sh`, `nohup-poll.sh`, and `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/{bash,pytest,node}-runner.sh` are deleted in the same commit as the callers are migrated. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, validate.sh, validate-phase.sh, the test-gate recording script, the per-commit test-gate hook, deps.sh, post-functions.sh, pre-bash-functions.sh, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, and every referencing test file (approximately 15) are updated. A tree-wide grep for the four removed script basenames plus the `scripts/runners/` path returns zero live references after the epic lands.
- Config migration + fail-closed guards: before launching, `start` validates (a) `commands.test_runner` is set and non-empty — if missing/empty, exits non-zero with a fail-closed error (no silently-green gate); (b) the resolved runner path does not reference `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/` — if it does (pre-migration host config), emits a migration warning with the recommended replacement value and exits non-zero. `commands.test_file_runner` uses a documented `{file}` placeholder that the per-file gate substitutes before invocation.
- Host shim compatibility: after upgrading the plugin, a host project that runs `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start` with an outdated shim receives an actionable error directing the user to run `dso update-artifacts`, not a raw `command not found`. The plugin version is bumped in the same commit so existing `update-artifacts` detection triggers on next invocation. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md documents this upgrade path.
- Value validation (sprint-executable): before closing the epic, a canary worktree runs one representative test-gate cycle under (a) the legacy test-batched path and (b) the new background primitive, on the same commit. The epic closure comment records both tool-call counts and total tokens consumed from dispatch to verdict. Acceptance: the new path completes in ≤3 tool calls (1 dispatch + ≤2 drains) and reduces tokens by ≥50% vs the legacy path on the same suite. Failure to meet either threshold blocks epic closure pending approach revision.

## Dependencies

- **87e9-0bc8** (Polyglot test-gate config layer) — depends_on. Epic 87e9-0bc8 defines `commands.test_runner` and `commands.test_file_runner`; this epic's launcher reads those keys. 87e9-0bc8 must land first.

## Approach

Introduce `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` with three thin subcommands: `start`, `status`, `kill`. `start` reads `commands.test_runner` via the existing `read-config.sh` shim, validates the resolved command (non-empty, not pointing at deleted `scripts/runners/`), acquires `flock` on `<state_dir>/.launch-lock`, checks for an existing liveness-verified run (kill -0 + cmdline-snapshot match), writes the new pid + cmdline snapshot atomically, then releases the lock and `exec`s the runner via `setsid` (detaching from the controlling terminal to survive orchestrator crashes) with stdout+stderr teed to `<state_dir>/progress.log`. On child exit, `start` writes exit code via write-then-rename and creates the `done` marker last (strict ordering: readers check `done` before reading `exit-code`). `--force` unconditionally executes the cmdline-verified kill-and-clear path. Session state dir is keyed on the sanitized Claude Code `session_id` captured by the session-start hook into `.current-session`; cross-session isolation is a natural consequence of the per-session dir layout. The session-start hook also registers a Stop hook cleanup: when a session ends with a `<state_dir>/pid` still present, the cleanup logs the orphan rather than killing it (detached test process gets to complete its work). CI fallback: when `.current-session` is absent, the launcher derives a session key from `$GITHUB_RUN_ID` → `$CI_JOB_ID` → `sha256(cwd)[:16]`, never from a shared static default. The orchestrator invokes `start` via `Bash(run_in_background: true)` for a `bash_id`, drains progress via `BashOutput(bash_id)`, polls `<state_dir>/done` for completion, and reads `<state_dir>/exit-code` as the authoritative result. The per-file test gate in the pre-commit hook invokes `commands.test_file_runner` with `{file}` placeholder substitution; when the key is unset, the per-file gate falls back to `$commands.test_runner <file>` with the file appended positionally. Migration is a single atomic commit: delete the 6 legacy scripts, add `test-run.sh`, update the 24 caller files, amend dso-config.conf with the new key defaults, and add test coverage for every start/status/kill + --force + stale-state + cross-session + duplicate-guard + cmdline-identity + CI-fallback + config-validation path. Because the atomic commit breaks bisect for regression recovery (the implementation plan notes this), the implementation plan includes a pre-merge validation run on a canary worktree.

**Atomic-commit rationale (defense against split recommendation)**: the Scope reviewer recommended splitting this epic into two — (A) introduce the primitive alongside legacy harnesses, (B) cut over callers and delete legacy. That split was considered and explicitly rejected by the practitioner in favor of a single atomic commit (option `c` in the Phase 1 flag-semantics decision). Rationale: (1) transitional coexistence of two harnesses doubles the test surface and creates a window where some callers invoke the new primitive and others still dispatch via `test-batched.sh`, producing inconsistent test gate semantics across the codebase; (2) the 24 caller updates are mechanical replacements validated by the zero-live-reference grep assertion in SC 8, bounding risk; (3) bisect risk is accepted in exchange for the cleaner cutover. Downside mitigations: pre-merge canary worktree validation (above), the Value validation SC gating closure on measured improvement, and commit revert as the rollback path.

## Scenario Analysis

- **setsid orphan survives session end** (`high`, `runtime`): detached test process continues after Claude session ends. Addressed by session-start Stop hook cleanup logging (not killing — the test completion is still valuable); cross-session state isolation means another session cannot accidentally adopt or collide with the orphan.
- **PID reuse collision after stale-state detection window** (`critical`, `runtime`): OS PID reuse could let `kill -0` succeed on an unrelated process. Addressed by identity-verified liveness — cmdline snapshot match required in addition to `kill -0`.
- **Exit-code write race with readers** (`high`, `runtime`): non-atomic write + ordering bug in reader. Addressed by atomic write-then-rename + strict `done` marker ordering (write exit-code first, `done` last; readers check `done` before reading exit-code).
- **SIGTERM grace period insufficient for pytest teardown** (`medium`, `runtime`): 5s insufficient for coverage finalization / fixture cleanup. Addressed by `commands.test_kill_grace_seconds` config key, default 30s.
- **progress.log unbounded growth during long suites** (`medium`, `runtime`): tens of MB on tmpfs could trigger ENOSPC. Noted in implementation plan — add `progress.log` size cap (e.g., 50 MB with head-truncation warning line). Not escalated to SC because it is an operational guard rather than a correctness property.
- **BashOutput drain misses final bytes after done marker** (`critical`, `runtime`): orchestrator treating BashOutput-empty as completion would misread test state. Addressed by the Orchestrator Invocation Contract SC: BashOutput is progress-streaming only; completion = `done` marker; result = `exit-code` file.
- **Concurrent start in same session without lock** (`high`, `runtime`): TOCTOU between liveness check and pid write. Addressed by the atomic-duplicate-launch-guard SC requiring `flock` on `.launch-lock`.
- **session-start hook not registered on upgrade** (`critical`, `deployment`): upgraded plugin with stale hook config fails to write `.current-session`. Addressed by the per-session-isolation SC's CI-and-degraded fallback precedence ($GITHUB_RUN_ID → $CI_JOB_ID → sha256(cwd)); never uses a silent shared default.
- **Atomic migration commit breaks bisect** (`medium`, `deployment`): single commit removes backward compatibility. Addressed in the Approach narrative (pre-merge canary worktree validation). Not escalated to SC because the risk is accepted — user explicitly chose no BC.
- **Host project `.claude/scripts/dso` shim not updated** (`high`, `deployment`): consumer projects need shim refresh. Addressed by the shim-refresh-precondition SC + plugin version bump in the same commit.
- **CI environment lacks session_id** (`critical`, `deployment`): CI is not a Claude session. Addressed by the per-session-isolation SC's CI fallback chain.
- **macOS /tmp cleanup wipes active state** (`low`, `deployment`): 3-day periodic cleanup removes stale state dirs. Mitigated by use of `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}` (macOS `$TMPDIR` is per-user, not auto-cleaned). Not escalated to SC — cleanup of truly idle state dirs is acceptable.
- **`commands.test_runner` missing or empty** (`critical`, `configuration`): silently-green gate allows broken code. Addressed by the config-migration-and-fail-closed SC.
- **`test_runner` references deleted runner scripts** (`high`, `configuration`): pre-migration config points at `scripts/runners/`. Addressed by the same fail-closed SC emitting a migration warning.
- **TMPDIR pointing to non-writable or non-executable fs** (`medium`, `configuration`): `setsid` / exec fails. Addressed by `start`'s pre-flight check on `<state_dir>` writability; exits non-zero with a clear error rather than hanging on `running`.
- **session_id contains path-unsafe characters** (`high`, `configuration`): path traversal via untrusted session_id. Addressed by the sanitization SC — strict charset + fall-through to hash.
- **`test_file_runner` assumes pytest-style file arguments** (`high`, `configuration`): per-language argument conventions differ. Addressed by the documented `{file}` placeholder convention in the config-migration-and-fail-closed SC.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered inline during the preceding analysis turn (MindStudio monitor-tool patterns, Claude Code issue #45717 SIGTERM propagation, claude-flow background commands wiki). Bright-line conditions that fired: novel pattern adoption + upstream-tool bug reliance.
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: **triggered — red team + blue team sub-agents dispatched** on the retrospective scrutiny pass after user challenge. 17 scenarios generated, 17 surviving blue-team filter (none rejected — all judged possible, actionable, distinct, high-confidence). Eleven of seventeen surfaced genuine spec gaps that drove this revision.
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: yes — user directed retroactive scrutiny run after initial brainstorm:complete tag was applied without dispatching sub-agents.
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered.
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered — no external integration signals (Claude Code internal primitive migration).
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered by hook behavioral logic changes (session-start hook). Prior-art not searched separately — the ecosystem analysis from earlier in this session (MindStudio, claude-flow) covered the relevant prior-art domain.
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: not applicable (primitive migration, no volume axis).
- **Initial brainstorm:complete violation**: recorded as bug `3dae-af1a`. Tag was applied without sub-agent dispatch; retroactively removed, scrutiny pipeline executed, SCs revised in response to surviving scenarios, this log corrected.
